commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
SLA-52: RACI-aware Jira sync with three-tier SLA escalation (#71)

* SLA-52: RACI-aware Jira sync with three-tier SLA escalation

- Add RACI mapping per candidateGroup in jira-sync-config.json
  (R/A/C/I roles, SLA durations, escalation chains per PRD REQ-NFR-006)
- Outbound sync embeds RACI assignment in Jira issue description
- SLA monitor loop (30s): 80% warning → comment + sla-at-risk label,
  100% breach → escalation issue (priority=High) assigned to A role,
  3+ chronic breaches → escalate to Oversight (3rd Line of Defense)
- Inbound sync notifies I (Informed) roles on task completion
- Crash recovery rebuilds SLA deadlines from Jira issue timestamps
- Dashboard shows live SLA progress bars, warning/breach counts
- Fix: Tasklist v1 filter uses numeric processDefinitionKey (not BPMN string)
- Fix: Jira search migrated to POST /rest/api/3/search/jql (GET deprecated)
- Fix: Task name newlines stripped from Jira summary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* SLA-52: Address PR review findings — crash recovery, JQL filter, input validation, XSS

- Recover instanceBreachCount from Jira escalation issues on restart (MEDIUM)
- Exclude sla-escalation issues from inbound sync JQL to prevent re-processing (MEDIUM)
- Throw on malformed/zero ISO 8601 durations instead of silent 4h default (MEDIUM)
- Rename misleading taskName variable to originIssueKey (LOW)
- Sanitize all dynamic data in dashboard innerHTML with esc() helper (LOW)

commit.message:
fix: use gunicorn --chdir to avoid backend package import issue

backend/ has no __init__.py so gunicorn couldn't import backend.server
as a dotted module. --chdir /app/backend lets gunicorn import server
directly from that directory instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: keyboard shortcuts — archive, board switch, new item, help overlay (#99)

* feat: keyboard shortcuts — archive, board switch, new item, help overlay (#91)

- Add useKeyboardShortcuts hook with input-focus suppression for single-key shortcuts
- A key toggles completed items archive dialog
- Ctrl/Cmd+1-9 switches between boards by position
- N key opens create item modal
- ? key toggles shortcut help overlay with categorized list
- Shortcut hints shown in board switcher dropdown (Ctrl+1, Ctrl+2, etc.)
- Refactor Ctrl+Shift+S handler from #90 to use the shared hook
- 27 new tests: 11 for hook, 8 for help overlay, 8 for board integration

Refs #91

* fix: handle shifted characters in keyboard shortcut hook

The ? shortcut did not fire on real keyboards because pressing ?
requires Shift, resulting in shiftKey:true. The modifier check
rejected it since the shortcut definition had no shift:true.

Now: for single-key shortcuts without Ctrl, the Shift state is
ignored since e.key already reflects the shifted character.
The strict Shift check is only enforced when Ctrl is held
(to distinguish Ctrl+S from Ctrl+Shift+S).

Added tests for shifted-character shortcuts and Ctrl+Shift
disambiguation. Updated kanban-board test to use realistic
shiftKey:true for the ? shortcut.

Refs #91

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implements quarter-end snapshot diffing to compute portfolio returns
from SEC 13F holdings data. Handles new/exited positions, unresolved
CUSIPs (confidence scoring), and options exclusion. Includes cumulative
return compounding and nearest-price fallback for non-trading days.

22 tests cover hand-verified returns, edge cases, and integration.

the quarterly returns module to provide comprehensive fund screening
capabilities including annualized return, Sharpe ratio, S&P 500
correlation, max drawdown, HHI, top-5 concentration, and turnover.

30 tests covering all metrics, edge cases, and insufficient data handling.

Implement screen_funds() for filtering and sorting funds by computed

with four named screens (top_performers, contrarian, concentrated,

feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat(templates): add TemplatePayloadInfo and restrict tx validation to apply payloads (#857)

- Add **TemplatePayloadInfo** class with `supportsTransactions` field so payloads can
  declare transaction compatibility via `TemplatePayload.getInfo()`
- Implement `getInfo()` in **TemplateString**, **TemplateVoid**, and test payload stubs
- Add `checkPayloadTransactionSupport()` and `getExplicitTransactionSupport()` helpers
  to **AbstractTemplateLoadedChange** for centralized transaction-support checking
- Restrict transaction-support validation to **apply payloads only** — configuration
  is shared state and rollback is a compensating action, neither represents an actual
  operation against a target system
- `warnIfAllPayloadsSupportTransactions()` now inspects only apply payloads in both
  **SimpleTemplateLoadedChange** and **MultiStepTemplateLoadedChange**
- Add **PayloadTransactionSupportValidationTest** with 10 cases covering error, warning,
  and not-checked paths for both simple and multi-step templates

commit.message:
Fix character name insertion creating spaces instead of formatted names

The previous approach used range.insertNode() which inserted the character
name div inside the current block element, creating nested divs that
rendered as spacing gaps. Now inserts as sibling divs at the editor's
top level by walking up to find the direct child block, then using
insertBefore to place the character name and dialogue divs after it.

Character names use var(--stage-gold) which adapts to theme:
- Dark theme: bright yellow (#e8c547)
- Light theme: dark mustard (#b8941a)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement fastcausal package (Phases 1-5)

Complete implementation of the unified causal discovery package
consolidating cda_tools2, fastcda, dgraph_flex, and tetrad-port:

- Phase 1: Package skeleton with pyproject.toml, dependencies, entry points
- Phase 2: FastCausal class, search (PC/FGES/GFCI), SEM, transforms,
  knowledge handling, edge parsing/dedup, bundled boston dataset
- Phase 3: Node styling (fnmatch), single/multi-graph display and save
  with shared neato layout positions
- Phase 4: Config-driven batch pipeline (parse, batch, paths, report,
  metrics), CLI wired to all pipeline modules, v4→v5 config migration
- Phase 5: 130 tests across 10 test files (119 pass, 11 skip for
  optional networkx)

commit.message:
Fix: convert monitor.js dynamic import to static to eliminate TDZ crash

Both DID accounts called await import('./monitor.js') concurrently,
creating a race window where the second account could access _serverPromise
while the module was still evaluating (temporal dead zone). Static import
ensures monitor.ts is fully initialized before any startAccount call runs.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add monocle layout, fullscreen/maximize, and floating windows

Three new window management features:

- Monocle layout: all windows fill the output, only focused visible.
  Toggle with Alt+M or 'dinatorctl layout monocle/column'.

- Fullscreen: windows fill the entire output without gaps, raised above
  tiled windows. Handles both IPC toggle (Alt+F, 'dinatorctl fullscreen')
  and client xdg_toplevel fullscreen/unfullscreen protocol requests.

- Floating: windows leave the tiling layout and float centered at 2/3
  output size. Toggle with Alt+V or 'dinatorctl float'.

Also adds maximize_request/unmaximize_request handlers (ack the state
in a tiling WM), floating/fullscreen flags in list-windows output,
layout-changed IPC event, and Layout::name() trait method.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 修复思考过程导出时仍包含在消息内容中的问题

修复claude-chat-python导出时包含思考过程的问题。根本原因为思考内容同时存储在thinking字段和content字段（带[思考]前缀），导致用户选择不包含思考过程时，思考内容仍出现在导出的Markdown文件中。

具体修改：
1. parser.py: 思考内容只存储在thinking字段，不添加到content字段
2. exporter.py: 新增_filter_thinking()方法，从消息内容中移除包含[思考]的行

修复后：
- 不包含思考过程(n): 导出的文件中无[思考]内容
- 包含思考过程(y): 思考内容仅在专门的思考过程代码块中显示

Phase 1 complete — all 7 screens are fully interactive:

- Order status timeline driven by real state (CREATED → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY → DELIVERED)

Architecture: ES modules, reactive state store, delegated event handling, toast notifications.
No backend — state is in-memory and resets on refresh.

commit.message:
fix: 에디터 인용문/코드 주석에 font-synthesis: style 추가 (#364)

## Summary
- Tiptap 에디터의 `blockquote`(인용문)와 `.hljs-comment`(코드 주석)에
`font-synthesis: style` 추가
- 한글 폰트에 이탤릭 글리프가 없어 `font-style: italic`이 무시되는 문제 수정
- 기존 markdown.svelte, comment-list.svelte, classic.svelte에는 이미 적용되어 있었으나
에디터에만 누락

## Test plan
- [ ] 에디터에서 인용문(blockquote) 작성 시 한글 이탤릭 렌더링 확인
- [ ] 에디터에서 코드 블록 내 한글 주석 이탤릭 렌더링 확인

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Redis wait loop from app entrypoint

Redis check was failing due to missing tools in the container.
Let the app handle Redis reconnection natively instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add inline re-moderation UI on content show page (#1672)

* Add inline re-moderation UI on content show page

Moderators can now change the status of already-approved or rejected
submissions directly from the content show page, without returning to
the moderation queue. Each submission in the Feeds list gets a "Change"
button that expands an inline form (hidden by default) with Approve and
Reject actions. The moderate action now redirects back to the referring
page so the flow works naturally from both the queue and the content page.

Closes #1667

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Use redirect_back_or_to instead of soft-deprecated redirect_back
- Remove unnecessary comment block from _moderation_actions partial
- Move max-w-sm constraint to the show_details layout, not the partial
- Add stop() to toggle controller and use it on the Reject button to
  prevent click event propagation to outer nested toggle controllers
- DRY up _card.html.erb moderation actions using the shared partial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Prevent open redirect via Referer header

Pass allow_other_host: false to redirect_back_or_to so that a
crafted Referer pointing to an external host is ignored and falls
back to submissions_path rather than following the attacker-controlled
URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: multi-note vault with tabbed editor and wiki-links (#2)

* feat: add multi-note vault with tabbed editor, wiki-links, and copy-as-markdown

Replace the single journal file with a full directory-based notes vault per
campaign (Obsidian-style). Each campaign gets a folder of markdown files with
arbitrary nesting.

Backend:
- NotesService: vault CRUD (notes, folders, move/rename, migration)
- NotesEndpoints: REST API for vault operations and session log designation
- AppendEntryToJournal writes roll results to the designated session log note
- Legacy single-file journals auto-migrate to vault on first load

Frontend:
- Collapsible sidebar with animated slide-out file tree
- Tabbed note editor with instant tab switching via content cache
- [[Wiki-link]] syntax with fuzzy autocomplete and clickable navigation
- Copy-as-markdown buttons on all roll result panels (Fate, Scene, Random
  Event, Meaning, Dice) with hover-reveal pattern
- Auto-save with debounce, stale cache invalidation after rolls

Bug fixes:
- Sidebar toggle button no longer overlaps header text (dynamic positioning)
- Create note/folder input auto-focuses immediately
- Session log badge updates reactively in file tree (computed, not static)
- Roll results correctly append to newly-designated session log

Tests: 16 new NotesService tests covering CRUD, path traversal protection,
migration, tree listing, and session log append flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review, fix collapse buttons, add docstrings

Bug fixes:
- Fix roll panel collapse buttons (collapsedIds was plain Set, now reactive)
- Fix path traversal in MigrateIfNeeded (use ResolveSafePath)
- Check WriteNote return values in EndpointHelpers (throw on failure)
- Update SessionLogPath when parent folder is moved/renamed
- Flush pending note saves before campaign switches (prevent data loss)
- Fix wiki-link disambiguation for duplicate basenames (use path|alias form)
- Replace global DOMPurify hook with scoped ADD_ATTR config
- Add keyboard accessibility to wiki-links (href, role, tabindex)
- Disambiguate tab names when filenames collide (show parent folder)
- Fix Tailwind v4 important modifier syntax (postfix !)
- Add Space keydown handler to file tree nodes
- Fix folder delete tab cleanup for exact path matches
- Fix currentColor casing in CSS for stylelint compliance
- Add type="button" to all copy-as-markdown buttons

Documentation:
- Add JSDoc docstrings to all functions in useNotes, useRollMarkdown,
  NoteEditor, NotesSidebar, WikiLinkAutocomplete, NoteTabBar,
  MarkdownBlock, and JournalView

Tests:
- Add path traversal rejection test for MigrateIfNeeded

- Wiki-link rendering: replace regex pre-processing with markdown-it inline

- NotesEndpoints: catch InvalidOperationException from path traversal and
  return 400 instead of letting it bubble as 500
- NoteTreeNode: add focus-within visibility for action buttons, remove unused

- Copy-as-markdown buttons: add focus:opacity-100 across all mythic panels

Add automatic AI tagging, fix feed sources, and add sitemap support

- Add auto-tagger module that runs AI tagging and tag discovery
  automatically during background feed refresh (with cooldowns)
- Add updateArticleTags() for efficient targeted tag updates
- Fix source library: update Reuters (sitemap), AP News (feedx.net),
  USA Today (web scraping), CNN/Semafor/Bleacher Report (web), and
  other broken feed URLs
- Add sitemap source type support and fallback chain in fetchSource
  (RSS → sitemap → web scraping) for sources missing type field
- Add sitemap-parser module for Google News Sitemap XML parsing
- Add CLAUDE.md project instructions

- Convert all TypeScript enums to `const` objects + type aliases
  (unit, game, resource, enemy, building, tiles, tutorialStore)
- Remove unused imports/variables (ControlBar, TutorialOverlay,
  production, buildingRenderer, resourceNodes, useUnitSync, enemySpawner)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track IA: PagedKVCache MLX-native (remove numpy backing)

Replace _k_np/_v_np float32 numpy arrays with _k_pool/_v_pool mx.array.
append() uses mx.concatenate block splicing + mx.eval() to materialise
the lazy graph after each call, preventing O(N) graph depth in decode loops.
gather() uses MLX gather indexing (pool[blk_idx].reshape(-1, H, D)).
Remove _invalidate_cache(), _k_pool_cached, _v_pool_cached.

Tests: update test_dual_pool_construction and test_append_single_token
to use _k_pool; add test_append_no_numpy_roundtrip. 351 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use server-side cookie for face verification persistence

Replace localStorage approach with an httpOnly cookie set via
POST /api/me/face-verify after successful scan. Layout reads the
cookie server-side on every request — zero face prompt on refresh
or new tab as long as cookie is valid (8 hours).
